title: Notational Intelligence, Linus Lee | Compile 26
summary: Linus Lee explores notational intelligence: how the representation systems we use to write down ideas shape our thinking. He discusses what makes good notation (abstraction, suggestiveness) and proposes that deep learning models could discover or invent entirely new notational systems to represent concepts more effectively.
writeup: Linus Lee presents notational intelligence: the idea that the symbolic and conceptual notation used to express a problem shapes how the problem is reasoned about. The talk identifies properties of good notation, including abstraction and suggestiveness, and argues these properties determine how effectively ideas can be manipulated. Lee then proposes that deep learning could discover or invent new symbolic systems for representing ideas, extending notation beyond forms designed by humans.
